title: Add per-session trade count cap per market slug — ban re-entry after N total entries

context.problem: Tonight the bot placed 9 sequential trades on mlb-cws-kc-2026-04-10 (all losses, -$145.46) and 9 on mlb-hou-sea-2026-04-10 (2 wins, -$89.16). There is no mechanism to stop re-entry after repeated losses on the same market. MAX_TRADES_PER_MARKET=1 only prevents concurrent positions — it does not limit cumulative entries in a session.
evidence: CWS-KC: 9 entries in one session. Entry prices descended: 0.62, 0.39, 0.33, 0.23, 0.18, 0.09, 0.06, 0.06, 0.05. Model kept recommending BUY_YES as price cratered.
The falling-knife pattern: bot follows the market down, buying the same losing team repeatedly
context.design: Add a session-level dict in bot_core that tracks total trade opens per slug. At entry gate time, query the DB for count of trades opened on this slug today. If count >= threshold, reject the entry with a clear log reason. Threshold: configurable via .env as MAX_SLUG_ENTRIES_SESSION, default 3.

objective: After MAX_SLUG_ENTRIES_SESSION total entries (open + closed) on a market slug in the current session, block further entries on that slug for the rest of the session.
forbidden_actions: Change exit logic or TP/SL thresholds
required_work: 1. In bot_core.py, add a DB query inside check_entry_gates() (or immediately before calling it in the bridge loop) that counts total entries for the given slug today (both open and closed).
2. If count >= MAX_SLUG_ENTRIES_SESSION, return a gate rejection with reason='session_slug_cap_exceeded:{count}>={cap}'.
3. Add MAX_SLUG_ENTRIES_SESSION to .env with default value 3.
4. Add the env var read to risk.py (or bot_core.py wherever appropriate — keep consistent with existing gate pattern).
5. Log the rejection with the same format as other gate rejections: 'BRIDGE GATE REJECT [check_entry_gates] slug=... reasons=[...]'.
6. The count must include both open and closed trades for today's session — do not count only closed.
acceptance_criteria: After 3 entries on a slug today, a 4th entry attempt is rejected with 'session_slug_cap_exceeded' reason
The rejection is logged in the same format as other check_entry_gates rejections
No files outside allowed scope are modified
python -m py_compile core/risk.py passes if risk.py is modified
verification_required: Show the exact code path added
Confirm where in the gate sequence this check runs
Confirm whether count includes open-status trades in addition to closed
Run py_compile on every changed file
State whether restart is required for this fix to take effect
